# ThreadSanitizer suppressions for the vanillapdf Python bindings.
#
# We LD_PRELOAD libtsan into a stock CPython that was NOT built with
# -fsanitize=thread. TSan derives happens-before from instrumented atomics/locks,
# so against an uninstrumented libpython it cannot see the GIL (or CPython's
# internal locks) as synchronization and reports interpreter-internal accesses as
# races even though the GIL serialized them -- false positives from missing
# instrumentation, not interpreter bugs. (CPython also documents a few genuinely
# benign races; both are why CPython itself ships Tools/tsan/suppressions.txt,
# the basis for the entries below.)
#
# Suppressing interpreter internals lets this job fail only on real races in the
# binding (src/vanillapdf_native) or the vanillapdf library exercised through it
# -- which is what matters now that #40 releases the GIL around native calls.
# Grow these from CI output; keep them scoped to interpreter/runtime internals,
# never to our own code (_vanillapdf / libvanillapdf).

# Broad: races whose access originates inside the (uninstrumented) CPython
# shared library. The exact soname varies (libpython3.13.so ...); the match is a
# substring.
called_from_lib:libpython

# Interpreter object model / allocator internals (GIL-serialized).
race:_Py_Dealloc
race:_Py_DecRef
race:_Py_IncRef
race:_PyObject_Malloc
race:_PyObject_Free
race:_PyObject_Realloc
race:_PyMem_Malloc
race:_PyMem_Free
race:gc_collect
race:tstate_delete_common
race:_PyRuntimeState
